entry.title: Unable to debug Third Party ALES Process flow in SM58
entry.content:
Hi Team,

To troubleshoot the third-party ALES process flow in the same SAP instance, we followed the steps outlined in the SAP Help Portal article "How to debug Third-party ALES process flow | SAP Help Portal." However, when we accessed SM58, we noticed an entry for the function module PUR_ORDER_CREATE_VIA_SD_EVENT. Yet, when we click on this entry and select "Debug LUW," it disappears from the screen.

Could anyone please assist ? Are there any additional steps we need to take to debug this scenario?

Regards,

Mythily

entry.title: Re: Clean core _Is appending fixed domain values to stand...
entry.content:
Hello,
You can set the ABAP language version for the append domain in ADT to "ABAP for Cloud Development" to make it clean core compatible. "ABAP for Cloud Development" repository objects are safe regarding future system upgrades.
Regards,
Patrick

entry.title: Express Node.js Multi-Tenant Backend Application with XSUAA on BTP - Non CAP based
entry.content:
Hi community,

I have created a Multi tenant SAAS application (Non-CAP NodeJS based) and have deployed this app to SAP BTP in the Provider subaccount. Along with the application I have the saas-registry instance and xsuaa instance created in my Provider subaccount. I am also able to subscribe to this application from the Consumer subaccount.


Current Status: So far, I have successfully implemented application subscription from tenant subaccounts using the saas-registry and an xsuaa instance. I’ve also implemented the necessary logic for subscribe and unsubscribe events in the backend.

Note: This is a pure backend application, and I am not using an Approuter, as there is no frontend component involved. The application is intended to be consumed by tenant-specific backend or frontend apps via API calls.

Problems:

  1. When I subscribe app in consumer subaccount, the xsuaa instance (using tenant-mode as "shared in provider subaccount) is not automatically created in consumer subaccount.
  2. To address this, I added event for getting dependencies while subscribing in backend the code is shown in below picture.
  3. Also added "url" for "getDependencies" in "appUrls" and dependencies detail in SaaS configuration file as shown below.
  4. After these changes, I tried subscribing to the app again from the consumer subaccount got an error shown below.

Asking for Suggestions:

  1. How can I achieve the automatic creation of an XSUAA instance in the consumer subaccount when subscribing to the app, to ensure full isolation between consumer subaccounts?
  2. If not then how can I achieve independent OAuth 2.0 Authentication for tenants specific to their subaccount?

entry.title: Auto Clearing of Vendor Open Items using BAPI "POSTING_INTERFACE_CLEARING" with residual items too
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

I got a requirement for my client to auto clear vendor open items through a background job report. It is an alternative to standard T-Code FB1K. In FB1K user able to perform clearing of open items only for one Account at a time and it is very tedious process to match open items and related invoices and clear them by creating Residual Items too if payment is not 0. So We came up with an Z report. 

Criteria to get Open Items (lt_open_items):

BSIK records: conditions: UMSKZ should be 'A', BSCHL should be '29' 

Criteria to get Related Invoices (lt_invoices):

BSIK records: conditions: BSCHL should be '31' 

Based on lt_open_items LIFNR, BUDAT, EBELN and I matching records with EKBE & MKPF to get EBELN and updating it in lt_invoices. Everything is fine & I am able to fetch records for example as shown below:
Open Items:

BukrsBelnrGjahrbuzeiumskzbschlwrbtrwaerslifnrebelnbudatbldat
10005000202310A29100000INRVEND0011234501-01-202402-01-2024
10005002202310A29200000INRVEND0021234601-01-202402-01-2024

Invoices Data:

BukrsBelnrGjahrbuzeiumskzbschlwrbtrwaerslifnrebelnbudatbldat 
10006000202310A31500000INRVEND0011234510-01-202411-01-20241st Invoice cleared against 100000 balance is 500000
10006001202310A34300000INRVEND0011234514-01-202415-01-2024500000 is cleared against 2nd invoice residual item 200000 is created
10006002202310A31200000INRVEND0011234518-01-202419-01-2024200000 is adjusted against 3rd invoice


Challenges which i am facing now:
1. I am processing each open Item and processing it for each Unique PO through BAPI but SY-SUBRC is becoming 0 but clearing not happening and residual items are not getting created obviously because somewhere I am loosing it.


ABAP LOGIC:
    TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_awkey,
             belnr TYPE belnr_d,
             gjahr TYPE gjahr,
           END OF ty_awkey.

    DATA: lt_awkey TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_awkey WITH EMPTY KEY.

    DATA: lt_return TYPE TABLE OF bapiret2,
          lv_line   TYPE i,
          lv_index  TYPE sy-tabix,
          lv_flag   TYPE flag.

    DATA: ls_header   TYPE bapiache09,
          lt_currency TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bseg,
          lt_ftclear  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ftclear,
          lt_ftpost   TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ftpost,
          ls_ftclear  TYPE ftclear,
          lt_blntab   TYPE TABLE OF blntab,
          lt_fttax    TYPE TABLE OF fttax,
          ls_ftpost   TYPE ftpost,
          lv_count(3) TYPE n VALUE '001',
          lv_simu     TYPE char1,
          lv_function TYPE funct_pi VALUE 'C',
          lv_mode     TYPE allgazmd VALUE 'N',
          lv_balance  TYPE wrbtr.


    SELECT bukrs, belnr, gjahr, buzei, umskz, bschl, blart,
           wrbtr, waers,lifnr, ebeln, budat, bldat
       FROM bsik
       INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_source_doc)
       WHERE lifnr IN @s_lifnr
       AND bukrs IN @s_bukrs
       AND budat LE @p_date
       AND umskz EQ 'A'
       AND bschl EQ '29'
      ORDER BY lifnr, ebeln, budat.
    IF sy-subrc NE 0.
      MESSAGE TEXT-t01 TYPE 'I'.
      LEAVE LIST-PROCESSING.
    ELSE.
      SORT lt_source_doc BY lifnr ebeln budat.
    ENDIF.

    SELECT bukrs, belnr, gjahr, buzei, umskz, bschl, blart,
           wrbtr, waers,lifnr, ebeln,budat
       FROM bsik
       INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_recipient_doc)
       WHERE lifnr IN @s_lifnr
       AND bukrs IN @s_bukrs
       AND budat LE @p_date
       AND bschl EQ '31'
      ORDER BY lifnr, ebeln, budat.
    IF sy-subrc NE 0.
      MESSAGE TEXT-t02 TYPE 'I'.
      LEAVE LIST-PROCESSING.
    ELSE.
      SORT lt_recipient_doc BY lifnr ebeln budat.
    ENDIF.

    IF lt_recipient_doc IS NOT INITIAL.

      SELECT bukrs, belnr, gjahr, awkey
       INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_bkpf)
       FROM bkpf
       FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @lt_recipient_doc
       WHERE bukrs EQ @lt_recipient_doc-bukrs
       AND belnr EQ @lt_recipient_doc-belnr
       AND gjahr EQ @lt_recipient_doc-gjahr.
    ENDIF.

    lt_awkey = VALUE #( FOR bk IN lt_bkpf (
                                belnr = bk-awkey(10)
                                gjahr = bk-awkey+10(4) ) ).

    IF lt_awkey IS NOT INITIAL.
      SELECT belnr, gjahr, ebeln
         INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_ekbe)
         FROM ekbe
         FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @lt_awkey
         WHERE belnr = @lt_awkey-belnr
         AND gjahr = @lt_awkey-gjahr.
    ENDIF.


    LOOP AT lt_recipient_doc ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<inv>).
      READ TABLE lt_bkpf INTO DATA(ls_bkpf)
           WITH KEY bukrs = <inv>-bukrs belnr = <inv>-belnr gjahr = <inv>-gjahr.
      IF sy-subrc = 0.
        DATA(lv_belnr) = ls_bkpf-awkey(10).
        DATA(lv_gjahr) = ls_bkpf-awkey+10(4).
        READ TABLE lt_ekbe INTO DATA(ls_ekbe)
             WITH KEY belnr = lv_belnr gjahr = lv_gjahr.
        IF sy-subrc = 0.
          <inv>-ebeln = ls_ekbe-ebeln.
        ENDIF.
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.

*    SORT lt_recipient_doc BY lifnr ebeln budat.

    LOOP AT lt_source_doc INTO DATA(ls_source).
      lv_balance = ls_source-wrbtr.
      lv_flag = abap_true.

      LOOP AT lt_recipient_doc INTO DATA(ls_recepient_doc) WHERE lifnr = ls_source-lifnr
                              AND ebeln = ls_source-ebeln AND wrbtr > 0 AND waers = ls_source-waers.
        IF lv_balance LE 0.
          EXIT.
        ENDIF.

        APPEND VALUE #( agkoa = 'K'
                            agkon = ls_source-lifnr
                            agbuk = ls_source-bukrs
                            agums = ls_source-umskz
                            selfd = 'BELNR'
                            selvon = ls_source-belnr
                            selbis = ls_recepient_doc-belnr
                            xnops = abap_true ) TO lt_ftclear.

        IF lv_balance GE ls_recepient_doc-wrbtr.
          lv_balance = lv_balance - ls_recepient_doc-wrbtr.
        ELSE.
          lv_balance = 0.
        ENDIF.
      ENDLOOP.

      CLEAR: lt_blntab, lt_fttax, lt_ftpost, ls_ftpost.
      IF lv_flag IS NOT INITIAL.
        APPEND VALUE #( stype = gc_header count = lv_count fnam = 'BKPF-BLDAT' fval = ls_source-bldat )  TO lt_ftpost.
        APPEND VALUE #( stype = gc_header count = lv_count fnam = 'BKPF-BUDAT' fval = ls_source-budat )  TO lt_ftpost.
        APPEND VALUE #( stype = gc_header count = lv_count fnam = 'BKPF-BELNR' fval = ls_source-belnr )  TO lt_ftpost.
        APPEND VALUE #( stype = gc_header count = lv_count fnam = 'BKPF-BLART' fval = 'AB' )             TO lt_ftpost.
        APPEND VALUE #( stype = gc_header count = lv_count fnam = 'BKPF-BUKRS' fval = ls_source-bukrs )  TO lt_ftpost.
        APPEND VALUE #( stype = gc_header count = lv_count fnam = 'BKPF-WAERS' fval = ls_source-waers )  TO lt_ftpost.
        APPEND VALUE #( stype = gc_item   count = lv_count fnam = 'RF05A-NEWKO' fval = ls_source-lifnr ) TO lt_ftpost.
        DATA(lv_sgtxt) = |Auto clearing|.
        APPEND VALUE #( stype = gc_item   count = lv_count fnam = 'BSEG-SGTXT' fval = lv_sgtxt )         TO lt_ftpost.
        APPEND VALUE #( stype = gc_item   count = lv_count fnam = 'BSEG-BSCHL' fval = '29' )             TO lt_ftpost.
        APPEND VALUE #( stype = gc_item   count = lv_count fnam = 'BSEG-WRBTR' fval = lv_balance )       TO  lt_ftpost.
        APPEND VALUE #( stype = gc_item   count = lv_count fnam = 'RF05A-NEWUM' fval = ls_source-umskz ) TO lt_ftpost.


        CALL FUNCTION 'POSTING_INTERFACE_START'
          EXPORTING
            i_function         = lv_function
            i_mode             = lv_mode
          EXCEPTIONS
            client_incorrect   = 1
            function_invalid   = 2
            group_name_missing = 3
            mode_invalid       = 4
            update_invalid     = 5
            user_invalid       = 6
            OTHERS             = 7.
        IF sy-subrc <> 0.
* Implement suitable error handling here
        ENDIF.

        CALL FUNCTION 'POSTING_INTERFACE_CLEARING'
          EXPORTING
            i_auglv                    = 'UMBUCHNG'
            i_tcode                    = 'FB05'
            i_sgfunct                  = 'C'
*           i_xsimu                    = space
          IMPORTING
            e_msgid                    = sy-msgid
            e_msgno                    = sy-msgno
            e_msgty                    = sy-msgty
            e_msgv1                    = sy-msgv1
            e_msgv2                    = sy-msgv2
            e_msgv3                    = sy-msgv3
            e_msgv4                    = sy-msgv4
          TABLES
            t_blntab                   = lt_blntab
            t_ftclear                  = lt_ftclear
            t_ftpost                   = lt_ftpost
            t_fttax                    = lt_fttax
          EXCEPTIONS
            clearing_procedure_invalid = 1
            clearing_procedure_missing = 2
            table_t041a_empty          = 3
            transaction_code_invalid   = 4
            amount_format_error        = 5
            too_many_line_items        = 6
            company_code_invalid       = 7
            screen_not_found           = 8
            no_authorization           = 9
            OTHERS                     = 10.

        IF sy-subrc <> 0.
        ENDIF.

        CALL FUNCTION 'POSTING_INTERFACE_END'
          EXPORTING
            i_bdcimmed              = abap_true
          EXCEPTIONS
            session_not_processable = 1
            OTHERS                  = 2.
        IF sy-subrc <> 0.
* Implement suitable error handling here
        ENDIF.

        READ TABLE lt_blntab INTO DATA(ls_blntab) INDEX 1.
        IF sy-subrc = 0.
          gs_final-belnr = ls_blntab-belnr.
          gs_final-bukrs = ls_blntab-bukrs.
          gs_final-msg   = 'Document is cleared successfully'.
        ELSE.
          IF sy-msgty IS NOT INITIAL.
            MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno INTO gs_final-msg
                    WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.

      ENDIF.
      CLEAR lv_flag.
    ENDLOOP.


Please let me know, If I am making mistake any where or I am taking wrong approach:
@ABAP @SAPABAP ABAP Development @FICO #

entry.title: Re: Process design and standardization for SAP Digital Supply Chain Cloud Solutions in SAP Signavio
entry.content:
Hi Hendrik,

For EWM the process content is available as part of the Best Practice content for S/4HANA through the Process Navigator in the SAP for Me platform.  For the Digital Supply Chain solutions that I have mentioned in the blog post, they are not available in that repository yet, which is why this separate content was produced as an installed package through Signavio.

Here is a link that will help you find the content for the EWM processes.  You would need to select the "Supply Chain" LoB from the list on the left, then you will be able to see all of the scope items that are relevant to EWM.

https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-055/2023-FPS02?region=DE

Good luck!

Carson

entry.title: Re: Extract ECC
entry.content:
I would say that the best approach will be the native DBMS tool. Almost every DBMS engine have a tool to export data, examples:

  • MSSQL: bcp and SQL Server Import and Export Wizard
  • ORACLE: data pump

entry.title: Event when Workitem is generated for a User
entry.content:
Hi All, 

We are looking for a possibility to Trigger some additional Functionality whenever a Workitem is generated for a user(Irrespective of Workflows, Tasks). Information about the User who should Process the Work item must be available.

This functionality should call an External UI Application via Proxy to Notify the Application about a new Workitem for the user. 

Is there a way to achieve this ?

Br,

Ketan

entry.title: Re: why Crystal Reports doesn't support Print Dialog to b...
entry.content:
Did you enable the CR Viewers Print Button?

You can also enable the Export Button.

If you want you can hide CR's print and export button and place your own buttons to do both through code.

Just remember you cannot print to PDF directly using CR's print button, I believe this is what you want to do using your own button, export then print to PDF.

Unfortunately there is no Print button click event:

So your first suggestion to use your own button is the only option for you.

Also be aware the export is formatted for their default printer unless you add dialog boxes for them to select their printer first and then export and then print the PDF using WEB PDF API's.

Hope that's clear now...

entry.title: Analytical Insights in Joule – Setup Guide
entry.content:
We are happy to announce the General Availability of the 4th Joule conversational pattern, the most requested service: the Analytical Insights capability with Joule. This release will help our Joule users ask analytics questions and obtain the required information from the SAP Analytics Cloud tenant.

Image 1

You can take a look at the demo of the analytics pattern here or you can always look at the blog post by @Flavia_Moser: Introducing Analytical Insights in Joule: Empowering Smarter Decisions, Instantly

After the wonderful video demo, let’s explore how to activate it within your landscape. First, it’s important to understand Joule’s overall strategy – a unified user experience. Please take a few minutes to explore it yourself if you are just getting started.

****************************************************************************

This blog post is a series for Joule Activation:

  1. SAP Business AI – Overview for all !!! (Includes information on Joule-supported data Centers, Different scenarios during Joule setup, etc.)
  2. Joule - Unified Setup: Bridging Simplicity and Performance (Demo Video to understand the Unified Setup, Joule basics & architecture, how to set up Joule, etc...) 
  3. Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide
  4. Joule Setup – End-to-End Setup Guide (for all Line of Business) Unified Approach
  • Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Setup Guide
  • SAP Digital Supply Chain
    • Joule for SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP) – Setup Guide
  • Joule – Getting Started with Document Grounding - setup guide
  • Activate Joule with SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Mobile Start
  • Joule in SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite – Setup Guide

👉Joule in SAP Analytics Cloud – Setup Guide – you are here

Blogs - Work in Progress (WIP):

  • SAP SuccessFactors Joule Activation (WIP)
  • SAP Digital Supply Chain (WIP)
    • SAP Asset Performance Management
    • SAP Product Lifecycle Management
    • SAP Digital Manufacturing
  • SAP Concur
  • SAP Ariba
  • SAP LeanIX
  • SAP Sales Cloud v2
  • and more to be added towards General Availability

****************************************************************************

Pre-requisites:

  • Joule Entitlements
  • SAP Analytics Cloud should be on the fast-track version (version 2025.7.0 and above; if you are still on version 2025.2, you still need to wait for your tenant’s updates)
  • Your SAP Analytics Cloud utilizes SAP Cloud Identity Services or SAML SSO, with your email address functioning as your login method. For further details, refer to SAP Note 3601035.

If you need to make any changes to your authentication with SAML SSO, you can refer to this help page: Enable a Custom SAML Identity Provider.

  • SAP Analytics Cloud – OAuth slot available for Joule setup

You can find the information in your SAC by going to System -> Administration -> App Integration and checking the Configured Clients section. If you see the “+ Add a New OAuth Client” button, then the prerequisites are fulfilled.

  • The Just Ask feature is enabled in your SAC tenant

(In case it’s not active, you can activate it via the System -> Administration -> Default Appearance -> and check that the Just Ask toggle under the Conversational Analytics.)

Hint: Always refer to the SAP Help Page, as they are updated regularly, and you can find the links here for :

- Integration with SAP Analytics Cloud

Integration Process:

In the current release of Analytical Pattern in Joule, at least one existing Joule setup is required with any line of business (LoB), such as SAP SuccessFactors, SAP S/4HANA Cloud, or SAP Build Work Zone. Joule can also be used in stand-alone mode. Please note that Joule is not currently available in your SAP Analytics Cloud tenant; this functionality is on our roadmap.

We have found that SAP customers already have Joule activated in most cases; therefore, we will address both options.

Image 2

Let us have a look at the steps involved for the integration:

  1. Integrating Joule
    • Scenario 1: Analytical Insights, a new setup
    • Scenario 2: You have an existing Joule setup, and a formation has been created
  2. Post-integration checks
  3. Joule test scenarios

Now that we have a process in place, we are ready to get started:

  1. Integrating Joule

Scenario 1: Analytical insights, a new setup

As mentioned above, for the new Joule setup with SAP Analytics Cloud, please ensure you select SAP Build Work Zone during the Booster process if you plan to activate Joule only for analytical insight capabilities.

  • You can follow the steps in this blog - Joule Setup – End-to-End Setup Guide (for all Line of Business) Unified Approach
    • Only skip the following steps
      • Step 3a. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Maintain Extensions on SAP BTP
      • Step 3b. SAP SuccessFactors - Extension Center
      • Step 3c. Validate your System Registration in SAP BTP
    • Follow all the steps apart from the ones listed above and focus on the steps below:
      • In step 6 - Activate Cloud Foundry and Create a Space. Required only if you are looking to activate Joule with SAP Build Work Zone
      • In step 10 – Select Integration and Select Capabilities – please select SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Build Work Zone

Once you’ve completed the Joule Booster process, you should be able to see the Joule formation in Ready status.

If you wish to access Joule in SAP Build Work Zone, please proceed with the activation process by following the blog:  Activate Joule with SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Mobile Start.

This completes the Joule activation for analytical insights.

 

Scenario 2: You have an existing Joule setup, and a formation has been created

In this section, we will show you how to activate analytical insights in the context of an existing Joule formation.

Within your SAP BTP Global Account, navigate to System Landscapes, click on Formations, and search for the Joule formation you have already set up. In my case, I am using one of the existing formations, which has multiple SAP products connected to one Joule instance, as shown below. You can click on Include System.

Image 3

In the new window, you may filter it only for SAP Analytics Cloud or select the desired system for Joule integration and click on Next.

Image 4

Select the option “Enable Capability Deployment” and click on Review.

Image 5 

Review the details, and if all is good, you may click on Include.

Image 6 

The process may take 5 to 8 minutes to complete, and you should be able to see the synchronizing option, as shown below. 

 Image 7

Once the status turns green and Ready, we are good to use Joule with Analytics capabilities.

Image 8

This completes the Joule activation for analytical insights.

 

2. Post-integration checks

 Once you follow the steps above, you can validate if the BTP Destination is created in your SAP BTP Subaccount. To check this, navigate to your SAP BTP Subaccount and click on Connectivity -> click on Destinations.

You should be able to see a Destination named – “DYN_SAC_SSO” with some of the details as shown below.

 

Note: No edits/changes are required; it’s a check if the setup is completed.

 

Image 9

Second, also check if the OAuth Client is created in your SAP Analytics Cloud system as shown below.

 Image 10

3. Joule test scenarios

We are now ready to set up the test models in the SAP Analytics Cloud and prepare Joule to pick the required responses. To prepare your data models, please refer to the section “Index Your Data Using Just Ask for Analytical Insights”.

Once you have indexed your models, you should be able to see the Joule response as below – please adjust according to your data.

Example: Show me an analytical chart for sales based on the month

Image 11

The above screen is from the SAP SuccessFactors Joule system, and since we have a Joule setup in a Unified setup with other SAP products, the next screen is in SAP LeanIX as an example to demonstrate that the conversation continues for the users, as shown below.

Image 12

In a similar approach, you may use Joule with a unified experience in other SAP products that you have integrated in the same subaccount.

If you have any questions about the setup, you can email us at SAP_AI_RIG@sap.com with the subject line "Joule—Analytical Insights.” 

Happy Learning!!!

Regards,

Nagesh Caparthy

entry.title: Re: Changing the transaction status of a position flow wi...
entry.content:
1. Use the App "Mark Flows as Fixed - Legacy Data Transfer" to change the status of the relevant flows to 'Fixed' without creation of an accounting document.
  2. After position flow is fixed via "Mark Flows as Fixed - Legacy Data Transfer", the position flow will have status 'Fixed' in Treasury Position Flows, and the corresponding cash flow in the deal will have posting information 'Fixed (not relevant for posting)'. 

Also provided in the SAP KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3604775 

Thanks & regards.

entry.title: Re: Is there any way to use se38 report in SAP public cloud?
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

No, you can’t use SE38 or run custom ABAP reports in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

That’s because this version of SAP is designed to keep the core system “clean.” It doesn’t allow access to traditional tools like SE38, SE80, or SE93. You also can’t write or run your own ABAP programs directly in the system.

 

So what can you do instead?

It depends on what you were using SE38 for. Here are some common use cases and what to use in the cloud version:

If you used SE38 to...

In Public Cloud, use...

Run reports

Standard Fiori apps like Display Journal Entries or Trial Balance

Add logic or rules

Use the Manage Substitutions/Validations app or Business Rules

Get data out

Build Custom CDS Views and use them in reports or expose via OData

Write your own code

You’ll need to use SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) for custom apps or reporting

 

If you really need to use SE38 and custom ABAP, the Private Cloud Edition of S/4HANA might be a better fit — it gives you access to all the classic SAP tools.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: How do setup IAS login page for Onboardee and SSO page for Employee authentication?
entry.content:
The client has set up IAS and SSO for SuccessFactors. The expected behavior is that employees will log in via SSO, while onboardees and other users will use the IAS login process.

Current Configuration
The default authenticating identity provider is set to IAS. Conditional authentication is configured such that employee logging in with an email address ending in client domain (@xyz.com) are redirected to the SSO login page, while all other users are directed to the IAS login page where they authenticate using a username and password.
Real-time sync of onboardee is enabled in IAS

The client is not having any IP range for employee to configure in Conditional rule.

Current behaviour

All users(Employee and Onboardee) are redirecting to IAS login. Once they enter username or email address, employee redirect to SSO page and onboardee continue in IAS login page.

Client Requirement

When an employee accesses the SuccessFactors (SF) URL, they should be redirected directly to the SSO login page, bypassing the IAS login page. However, when an onboardee clicks the login link, they should be directed to the IAS login page for authentication.
Has anyone configured or designed a solution for a similar requirement?

Additionally, is the client's requirement feasible?

@jaideepshetty @zebashah @lukemarson @sushilgupta857 @abdulbasit 
SAP Single Sign-On SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding Identity Provisioning #authentication

entry.title: Re: Message server query failed with return code -14
entry.content: This seems to be wrong. Gui-Groups (without the "Ext. RFC-enabled" flag activated), can also be used for RFC logon. (Just tested with latest Kernel release 917 and JCo 3.1.12) The flag "Ext. RFC-enabled" only has an influence on whether NAT works or doesn't work. See note 593058, point 4b.

entry.title: Re: JDBC Adapter Fails to Parse Scalar Response from Stor...
entry.content: @DhrubS the adapter processing level is one spot where you do not have the capability of intercepting, and it's basic job is to run the statement and generate an XML representation (doesn't expect it, it's responsible for generating it) of the result for which the anonymous scalar value is impossible to name appropriately. You can look into a custom adapter that mimics the JDBC adapter, but frankly that's too much wasteful work when adjusting the stored procedure takes seconds.

Load all hierarchies to a dropdown widget. On selecting an item in dropdown, change the hierarchy of Account dimension.

entry.title: Ariba Buying and Invoicing Duplicate Invoice Check
entry.content:
I would like to perform a duplicate check on the first 16 characters of the Ariba invoice number in Buying and Invoicing.

We use Ariba Buying and Invoicing with SAP ECC back-end system.  When an invoice 1234567-MAINT-2025 was created, the ECC MM invoice record did not get created.  There were no errors in Ariba indicating that the invoice did not get created.  The Ariba PAY record had a scheduled status.  There was a previous invoice 1234567-MAINT-2024.  The ERP invoice number of the previous (2024) invoice got associated to the 2025 invoice.  The ECC invoice reference number is only 16 characters.

I understand that with MM invoice verification we could detect duplicate invoices like this, however, I would like to trap the error when the Ariba invoice is created before it is reconciled and sent to ECC.

Thanks in advance for your help.

entry.title: Choosing the Right Analytical Tool: A Comprehensive Guide for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Selecting the right analytical tool within the SAP S/4HANA Cloud framework can be pivotal to addressing your business needs effectively. Embedded analytics within SAP S/4HANA Cloud offers a suite of tools to answer diverse business questions, foster data-driven decision-making, and streamline operations. Here, we’ll delve into the factors that should guide your choice in selecting the most suitable embedded analytical tool for your organization and requirements. 

  1. Tailored Support for Diverse Needs
  2. Overview on all available analytical tools inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
    • Review Booklet
    • Multidimensional Analysis
    • Overview Page
    • Analytical List Page
    • Smart Business KPIs
    • Embedded SAP Analytics Cloud Story
    • SAP Analysis for Microsoft Excel
  3. Checklist
  4. Feature / Function / Capability focus
  5. Self-service learning

Tailored Support for Diverse Needs

The landscape of business analytics is vast, and having the correct tool set can significantly enhance business operations by:

  • Addressing specific business queries and needs.
  • Increasing user adoption and engagement with analytical tools.
  • Enabling business self-service for ongoing data insights into operations.

When navigating through SAP S/4HANA Cloud's embedded analytics offerings, consider the following factors to align with your business goals: 

  1. 🧠 Key User Availability & Expertise: Ensure that the users have the necessary skills and expertise to interact with the chosen tool.
  2. 🏢 Line of Business (LoB): Matching the tool to the specific needs of departments such as finance, sales, or procurement can lead to more insightful analysis.
  3. 📅 Report Type and Cadence: Determine whether you require mass reports, individual reports, or a mix; and establish the frequency of these reports (daily, weekly, monthly).
  4. 📊 Report Complexity: Evaluate the complexity regarding multiple KPIs and dimensions to choose a suitable tool.
  5. 🌐 Business Domain Coverage: Establish whether your needs involve one or multiple domains.

Furthermore, different personas use analytics to drive specific aspects of their roles, leveraging different types and levels of data to meet their unique objectives and contribute to the overall success of the company. This table outlines the personas, their roles related to analytics, and the recommended tools to support those functions on high level.

 

PersonaRole Description and ExamplesRecommended Product
Executive LeadershipHigh-level strategic decision-making, financial performance tracking, and overall business health assessment.Enterprise SAP Analytics Cloud or SAP Business Data Cloud 
Managers (e.g., Sales, Supply Chain)Sales performance tracking, pipeline management, identifying sales opportunities/inventory management, supplier performance, and logistics optimization.Embedded analytics in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Financial AnalystsBudget analysis, investment performance, managing the company's liquidity, funding, capital management, organize and control receivables and collections, etc.Embedded analytics in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Financial PlanningProvides overall financial planning and performance management insights.Enterprise SAP Analytics Cloud e.g. via Rapid FP&A for SAP S/4HANA 

Now, let's go deeper and let me provide you an overview on the analytical tools that SAP offers inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. More information can also be found here.

back to top

Review Booklet

As this is one of the new kids on the blog. I'd like to start with the Review Booklet. In essence, Review Booklets provide pre-configured, user-friendly business pages designed to address common and pertinent business inquiries for your day-to-day tasks right out of the box. They merge application logic and direction with built-in multidimensional analytics, enhanced further by advanced AI capabilities as they evolve. SAP ships already multiple Review Booklets with out-of-the-box content. We also know that our content never hits 100% of your requirements, thus it is possible to adjust the content via the Review Booklet Designer. An image or video says more that thousand words when it comes to data visualization, so please take a look at this example of a Review Booklet:

Key benefits of Review Booklet:

  1. Consolidated Reporting: Gather multiple reports into a single booklet format, streamlining access and enhancing information organization for more efficient review.
  2. Predefined Layouts for Business Queries: Utilize ready-made layouts tailored to address specific business questions, providing immediate insights without extensive customization.
  3. Guided Analysis with Multidimensional Insights: Experience guided and flexible analysis modes enriched by contextual actions and generic pivoting, seamlessly combining application logic with integrated multidimensional insights to support thorough exploration and understanding

back to top

Multidimensional Analysis

The second 'new' kid is the Multidimensional Analysis report. Multidimensional Analysis offers a flexible and interactive way to delve into complex datasets, enabling users to uncover insights more intuitively. It begins with an analytical query in its default view, serving as a starting point for exploration. From there, users can pivot and drill down into the data, adding dimensions, measures, and structures to rows and columns to tailor the analysis to specific needs. This approach is particularly beneficial for advanced users, facilitating deep data exploration and empowering them to discover trends and patterns that drive informed decision-making. For more info, check out this blog. Multidimensional Analysis will replace the reports based on the 'old' technology of Web Dynpro Grid in the future, so better get yourself familiar with it.

Key benefits of Multidimensional Analysis reports:

  1. Flexible and Interactive Exploration: Enjoy adaptability through interactive pivoting and drill-down capabilities, allowing users to dynamically manipulate data for deeper insights.
  2. Customizable Data Views: Begin with a default view of an analytical query and progressively enhance your exploration by adding various dimensions, measures, and structures to both rows and columns for tailored analysis.
  3. Advanced Data Exploration: Cater to advanced users seeking comprehensive understanding, offering powerful tools for extensive data investigation and analysis.

Figure: shows a Multidimensional Analysis on Income Statement and how to add a calculation on the fly.

back to top

Overview Page

An overview page is a unique SAP Fiori app designed to enhance user experience by offering a streamlined structure. Each overview page is composed of predefined cards of varying types displayed on a canvas beneath a filter bar. Adjustments made in the filter bar apply universally to all cards within the app, ensuring cohesiveness in data presentation. Users can interact with the cards by clicking on them, allowing navigation to additional apps such as reports, KPI details, or transactional applications.

Key benefits of Overview Page:

  1. KPI Cards for Immediate Insight: Deliver a quick and comprehensive snapshot of a domain by showcasing key performance measures, enabling users to grasp the overall picture swiftly.
  2. Aggregated Data Display: Present data in a consolidated format, streamlining information to enhance clarity and facilitate informed decision-making.
  3. Navigation Options: Enable deeper investigation through seamless navigation, positioning overview pages as an ideal entry point for comprehensive analysis.

Figure: Procurement Overview Page for Purchaser.

back to top

Analytical List Page

The Analytical List Page (ALP) is an integral component of SAP Fiori designed to transform business key performance indicators into dynamic analytical dashboards. These dashboards incorporate KPIs, charts, tables, and visual filters to provide insightful data visualizations. Powered by SAP UI annotations, ALP offers the flexibility to explore data from various angles, allowing for in-depth analysis of root causes and supporting timely decision-making. 

Key Benefits of ALP:

  1. Insight to Action is Key: Empower business users to process their work lists in context with their analytical findings, driving effective outcomes.
  2. Focus on Critical Issues: Easily highlight and address the most pressing concerns, and take immediate action without having to switch screens.
  3. Prioritize Efficiency: Sort and filter work lists to ensure that the most critical issues are prioritized and dealt with promptly.

Figure: Supervise Collection Worklist for Accounts Receivable Manager.

back to top

Smart Business KPIs (including Smart Business Runtime aka generic drill-down)

The KPIs are presented on tile surfaces using either simple numerical values or mini-charts, organized through the SAP Fiori Launchpad. Business users have the flexibility to select which KPIs to display on their personalized Launchpads, choosing from catalogs assigned to their roles. For deeper analysis, users can easily click on a tile to access detailed drill-down information. The Smart Business runtime environment provides access to detailed configurations of evaluations, enabling users to drill down and analyze KPIs in detail. The runtime is enhanced with smart controls, such as smart filters and smart charts, to further streamline the analytical process.

Key benefits of Smart Business KPI reports:

  1. Visual and Color-Coded KPI Representation: Display KPIs the SAP Fiori launchpad with simple numerical values, utilizing colors to offer immediate visual cues and enhance comprehension of performance levels.
  2. Instant Performance Overview: Provide an at-a-glance view of business performance, enabling quick assessments and timely decision-making.

Figure: Analysis section on the Fiori Launchpad for Accounts Receivables Managers opening the Smart Business generic drill-down app Overdue Receivables.

back to top

Embedded SAP Analytics Cloud Story

The SAP Analytics Cloud embedded version integrates seamlessly with the SAP Fiori UI of SAP S/4HANA Cloud and the so called Stories offer thereby an additional tool to analyze business data. Stories are designed to transform raw data into insightful visualizations, empowering users to enhance business performance and make informed, data-driven decisions including seamless navigation to and from other S/4HANA Cloud apps. These stories reveal hidden insights in your data by allowing the addition of text, images, and generating dynamic visualizations such as charts and graphs. Of course, SAP offers a large set of out-of-the-box content which can serve as a quick start and can be customized according to the requirements. 

Key benefits of an embedded SAP Analytics Cloud Story:

  1. Powerful Visualization and Feature-Rich Dashboards: Create dynamic and visually compelling analytical dashboards that enhance understanding and decision-making.
  2. Interactive Data Exploration: Easily explore data through linked analysis, page filters, chart filters, and input controls, providing a user-friendly experience for in-depth insights.
  3. Advanced Data Analysis: Utilize location analytics, perform calculations on the fly, and analyze multiple queries for comprehensive analysis.

Figure: Purchasing Spend Dashboard for Purchasers.

back to top

SAP Analysis for Microsoft Excel

There is also an option to analyze business data from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition using SAP Analysis for Microsoft Excel. However, this reporting tool is not embedded within SAP S/4HANA Cloud, but can be opened via an app from SAP Fiori Launchpad. 

Figure: Opening SAP Analysis for Microsoft Excel from Fiori Launchpad. 

back to top

Checklist

Now consolidating all the information above, I come to the following checklists that shall help preselecting possible tool candidates by ticking the checkboxes according to your needs:

back to top

Feature / Function / Capabilities focus

Now that I provided an holistic overview on all the available options to analyze your business data, I'd like to get into the details. Important to know is that the following tables show the strengths of each tool, there is no one fits all solution as different information requirements and perspectives require different capabilities. 

I will explain the one or the other capability to make sure that you understand these tables correctly. Please leave a comment if something is not clear.

entry.title: How 'Relaxed Flow' flag in Manage Recipes/Routings app affects Order Release and SFC quantities
entry.content:
In this blog, I will show you in detail how 'Relaxed Flow' flag in Manage Recipes/Routings app affects Order Release process and the released SFCs quantities.

When producing materials with 'Lot Size' > 1:

Upon Order Release, 'X' Shop Floor Controls (SFCs) are created, where 'X' is calculated as the 'Quantity to Release' divided by the order header Material 'Lot Size'.

Once created, each SFC is assigned a quantity equivalent to the respective material Lot Size. In the example above, for a Lot Size of 20, when releasing 100 as 'Quantity to Release', 5 SFCs of 20 quantity each are generated.

Partial order releases and releasing quantities smaller than the Material Lot Size are also supported.

The release behavior and final SFC quantities released are highly dependent on the 'Relaxed Flow' flag set in the routing within the Manage Routings/Recipes app:

In the example above, with a Lot Size of 20, if you perform three individual/partial releases with the following quantities: 15, 15, and 40, the result would be as follows:

- If 'Relaxed Flow' is NO: A brand-new SFC is released every time, quantity is not accumulated from one release to another, from one SFC to the next one.

Where:

    • 1st release of 15, created SFC D100743 of 15.
    • 2nd release of 15, created SFC D100744 of 15.
    • 3rd release of 40, created two SFCs D100745 and D100746 of 20 (the lot size) each.

- If 'Relaxed Flow' is YES: Quantity is accumulated from one release to another, from one SFC to the next one until it reaches the lot size.

Where:

    • 1st release of 15, created SFC D1006008 of 15.
    • 2nd release of 15, updated SFC D1006008 quantity to 20 and created SFC D1006009 of 10.
    • 3rd release of 40, updated SFC D1006009 quantity to 20 and created SFCs D1006010 of 20 and D1006011 of 10 remaining quantity.

You can learn more about 'Lot Size' > 1 and 'Relaxed Flow' at:

  • Handling SFCs with Lot Size > 1 in SAP Digital Manufacturing
  • Relaxed Flow Routings within SAP Digital Manufacturing

Do you like this post? Please let me know in the comments section what you think. Any feedback is highly appreciated.

Or, if you have any questions, please check SAP Community Q&A Area, or comment down below.

Thanks,
Manoel Costa

Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to determine the Feature a transport request is a...
entry.content:
The Transport Analysis application allows the user to search with the Transport Request ID and returns any matching entry which shows a wealth of information about the transport.

To access this application:

  1. Launch SAP Cloud ALM
  2. Click Implementation > Analytics and Reports > Cross-Project Overview
  3. Click Transport Analysis
  4. In Search, enter the Transport Request ID and press enter
  5. The results will return the transport, its title, status, the linked Feature, and the target tenant

entry.title: Re: Single Sign On - all Hybrid SAP landscape - One Identity
entry.content:
Hello Vasu;

For the Web based access on the on-premise systems you shouldn't have any major complexity. As the Web based uses HTTP protocol, it is compatible with SAML2 so you can centralize both your cloud solutions and on-premise(web based access) in IAS as proxy towards your Entra ID.

For the SAPGUI based access it gets a bit trickier but is also feasible. Different from the Web based which uses HTTP, SAPGUI access uses the SNC protocol which only supports Kerberos or X.509 certificates for SSO. In this case to centralize the SSO, you will need to use the Secure Login Service for SAPGUI, which is the replacement for SSO 3.0. This BTP service generates short-lived X.509 certificates so users can use it as the SSO token for SAPGUI. The BTP service supports SSO through IAS as proxy towards your Entra ID.

It works in a way that every user will need to authenticate against your corporate IDP to generate the certificates that will be valid between 1-12 hours(you can setup this in SLS for SAPGUI) if I'm not mistaken.

-For the cloud solutions you will need to refer to their product documents to see how it is implemented, but a lot of solutions already have wizards to execute the trust configuration towards IAS.
-For the Web based access on on-premise solutions (both Java and ABAP) this wiki should provide you some guidance: https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/security/3362974627.html
-For the SAPGUI SSO with SLS for SAPGUI please refer to https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/security/3362974627.html

Please refer to the reference architecture on Cloud leading authentication as well: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/refArchDetail/ref-arch-cloud-leading-authentication

Cheers!

entry.title: SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) for SAP Basis Professionals
entry.content:
This Blog is mainly written for the SAP Basis colleagues, Project Managers and other SAP consultants who are not much familiar with IBP. This blog will also provide some basis knowledge on supply chain planning operations.

SAP IBP is a next-generation supply chain planning tool hosted on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). It combines:

  • Predictive analytics powered by AI and machine learning
  • Cloud-based architecture for scalability
  • Real-time collaboration across supply chain teams
  • Seamless integration with SAP ERP and S/4HANA

 

Why is SAP IBP Gaining Popularity?

  1. Cloud Flexibility – No on-premise infrastructure to maintain.
  2. Advanced AI Forecasting – Improves demand planning accuracy.
  3. Integration with SAP S/4HANA – Enables real-time data exchange.
  4. End-to-End Visibility – Optimizes global supply chains efficiently.

 

  •  Most of the companies are now working to move from SCM system to cloud based IBP system.

 

There are multiple other cloud solution for supply chain planning and one of the most popular tool is Kinaxis RapidResponse.

 

Now lets start with How SAP Basis Professionals Work with IBP

 

Like All other SAP cloud applications, SAP Basis team will be working on the technical configuration, integration of IBP with on-premise and other cloud bases applications.

 

SAP Basis teams play a vital role in IBP system architecture and administration:

 

Below is the diagram we have taken from SAP IBP documentation guide which explains different connections we can have with SAP IBP.

SAP IBP technical diagram Basis

IBP is used mainly for time series based integration (long term planning) and Order based integrations (short term). Based on the long term or short term planning integration type is selected.

Lets understand the integration types

  1. Time-Series-Based Integration

Time-series planning organizes data into period-based key figures (e.g., weekly, monthly, yearly) and is used for forecasting, demand planning, and inventory optimization.

 

Most customers used Time series planning as it is used mainly for long term planning or to handle demand planning related scenarios

 

Timeseries based integration

You can refer blog for integration of CI-DS with ECC.

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-members/sap-erp-and-sap-ibp-technical-integration-using-ci-ds/ba-p/13579693

Below is the example of how IBP system will collect the data and perform the demand planning operations based on the historical data to create the forecasts.

Planning

Most of the Basis people are not aware of Planned Independent Requirement. PIR is used in SAP Demand Management to define anticipated demand for a product without being directly linked to sales orders. PIRs help businesses plan production based on forecasts rather than customer orders.

      2. Order Based Planning

Order-based planning focuses on real-time transactional data (e.g., sales orders, purchase orders, stock transfers) and is used for short-term execution and response planning.

Order based planning

Order based Planning connect ECC or S4HANA systems with IBP via cloud connector configurations.

 

With these two integrations types, Basis team can choose the different components they need to setup. 

If you have some experience in SAP Manufacturing processes you can refer some if the below IBP use cases

  1. Sales & Operations Planning (S&OP)

Use Case: A manufacturing company aligns demand forecasts with production capacity to avoid stock shortages.

  1. Demand Planning

Use Case: A retail chain predicts seasonal demand fluctuations using AI-driven forecasting.

  1. Inventory Optimization

Use Case: A pharmaceutical company minimizes excess stock while ensuring critical medicines are available.

  1. Supply Planning

Use Case: An automotive supplier optimizes raw material procurement based on production schedules.

  1. Response & Supply Execution

Use Case: A logistics firm adjusts supply chain routes dynamically based on real-time disruptions.

I believe if you have not started working on IBP yet, some of the above terms explained above will help you to get started.

entry.title: Kickstart Your Developer Journey – Get SAP-Certified in Low-Code/No-Code
entry.content:
New to development? No problem! SAP offers a certification and learning resources that help you build real business apps, workflows, and user experiences — without needing to be a coding expert. Using tools like SAP Build Apps, Work Zone, and Process Automation, you’ll be surprised how much you can do just by dragging, dropping, and configuring.

 

Simple Steps to Get Certified

 

Step 1: Learn at Your Own Pace Follow these beginner-friendly learning paths:

  • SAP Build Apps: Build apps visually without needing to write code. Learn how to connect data, create user-friendly pages, and define app behavior.
  • SAP Build Work Zone: Set up a digital workspace where users can access their apps and content in one place.
  • SAP Build Process Automation: Learn how to build simple workflows and automation tools to save time and reduce manual work.

Step 2: Practice What You Learn You’ll try a real-world simulation using everything you’ve learned. This hands-on practice is required before taking the certification exam and helps boost your confidence.

Step 3: Take the Certification Exam Once you feel ready, book your exam. Passing it proves your skills and gives you an official SAP Certification — a great addition to your resume!

 

Want to Keep Growing?

 

  • Watch tutorials on YouTube from SAP Developer Advocates.
  • Join local SAP CodeJam events to meet and learn from other developers.
  • Subscribe to SAP Learning Hub for more guided learning from SAP experts.

 

Whether you're starting your career or switching to tech, this certification is a great way to get hands-on with real business tools — and build your future.

entry.title: Re: How to throw errors from REST API calls (CAP CDS and...
entry.content:
Hi T1mey,
Thank you for your reply. I use Kotlin for Handlers and I use the req.err equivalent which is ServiceException. I use it as below

catch (e: RestClientResponseException) {
            log.error { "Failed. Response: ${e.statusCode} ${e.responseBodyAsString}" }
            if (e.statusCode == NOT_FOUND) {
                messages.errorCode("notFound")
            }
            messages.errorCode("error", e.responseBodyAsString)
            messages.throwIfError()
        }

entry.content: The user does not want to change the grouping by due date — it is a mandatory requirement for them. I have copied the program RFFOAVIS_FPAYM into a custom Z program and modified part of the code. It seems to be working well.

entry.title: Re: Table(s) containing Views and their table(s) information
entry.content: View definitions DD25L Stores view header definitions (basic info about views). View to base tables DD26S Defines base tables used in a view and their relationships (join conditions). View field mappings DD27S Field mapping from base tables to view fields. View selection conditions DD28S Selection conditions used in views (like WHERE clauses).
